author: Antigravity Agent
funFacts: Amargasaurus was characterized by two parallel rows of tall, spiky spines running down its neck and back, which may have supported a skin sail.
Amargasaurus was a relatively small sauropod (long-necked herbivore), measuring about 33 feet (10 meters) in length.
